text:
Market today: The mood is cautious, with tech and chip names facing headwinds. The real driver is a combination of AI-related debt selling off and Micron's warning, signaling potential cooling in your core sectors. Geopolitical tensions from Iran strikes add broader market uncertainty.

Your holdings: SOXL is a major concern, down -35.06% and leveraged; its "SELL" status demands attention. SPAL, also leveraged and new, is down -23.1% and needs review. QQQI, down -2.16%, is flagged for a TRIM, with its stop near. Remember, margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on further developments in AI-related debt markets and any ripple effects across the broader tech sector. Also, monitor the memory market's health following Micron's fall.

Bottom line: Prioritize aggressive risk management on your leveraged and underperforming positions, especially given the current tech headwinds.
plays: Review SOXL and SPAL immediately; their leveraged nature and significant drawdowns (-35.06%, -23.1%) demand a hard look at reducing exposure.
Trim QQQI at 52; its "TRIM" status and proximity to the stop suggest taking action.
Consider a new buy in RTX: BUY 9/12, entry 200.65-200.45, stop 200.13, given the geopolitical catalyst and strong scanner setup.
